question: What is the admission process?
answer: The admission process involves: 1) Online application submission through our portal, 2) Entrance exam (if applicable), 3) Merit list publication, 4) Document verification, 5) Fee payment and confirmation. The entire process takes approximately 4-6 weeks from application to confirmation.

question: What are the eligibility criteria for undergraduate programs?
answer: For undergraduate programs, candidates must have completed 10+2 (Higher Secondary) from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% aggregate marks (45% for reserved categories). Specific programs may have additional subject requirements - e.g., PCM for Engineering, PCB for Medical Sciences.

question: What is the last date for admission applications?
answer: The application deadline for the academic year 2025-26 is June 30, 2025, for regular admissions. Late applications may be accepted until July 15, 2025, with an additional late fee of Rs. 500. Management quota admissions close on August 15, 2025.

question: What documents are required for admission?
answer: Required documents include: 1) 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates, 2) Transfer Certificate (TC), 3) Migration Certificate, 4) Character Certificate, 5) Aadhaar Card, 6) Passport-size photographs (6 copies), 7) Caste certificate (if applicable), 8) Income certificate (for scholarship applicants), 9) Entrance exam scorecard (if applicable).

question: Is there an entrance exam for admission?
answer: Yes, for professional courses like Engineering (JEE/State CET), MBA (CAT/MAT/CMAT), and Medical (NEET), entrance exam scores are mandatory. For general undergraduate programs like B.A., B.Sc., B.Com., admission is based on 12th marks merit list. Some departments conduct their own aptitude tests.

question: How can I check my admission status?
answer: You can check your admission status by: 1) Logging into the admission portal with your application ID, 2) Calling the admissions helpline at 1800-XXX-XXXX (toll-free), 3) Visiting the admissions office in person during working hours (9 AM - 5 PM, Monday to Saturday).

question: Is there a management quota for admissions?
answer: Yes, 15% of seats are reserved under the management quota. Management quota admission requires a separate application form and higher fee structure. Selection is based on academic performance, entrance exam scores, and a personal interview conducted by the management committee.

question: Can I transfer from another college?
answer: Yes, lateral entry and transfer admissions are available for 2nd and 3rd year students subject to seat availability. You need a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from your current institution, transfer certificate, and must have completed equivalent coursework. Transfer applications are accepted between May 1 and July 31.

question: What undergraduate programs are offered?
answer: We offer the following undergraduate programs: B.Tech (CSE, ECE, Mechanical, Civil, IT), B.Sc (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology, Computer Science), B.A (English, History, Political Science, Economics, Psychology), B.Com (General, Honours, Computer Applications), BBA, BCA, B.Pharm, and B.Ed.

question: What postgraduate programs are available?
answer: Postgraduate programs include: M.Tech (CSE, ECE, VLSI, Structural Engineering), M.Sc (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biotechnology), M.A (English, Economics, Public Administration), M.Com, MBA (General, Finance, Marketing, HR, IT), MCA, and M.Ed. Duration is typically 2 years for all PG programs.

question: What is the duration of B.Tech program?
answer: The B.Tech program is a 4-year (8 semesters) full-time course. Each semester is approximately 6 months. Students must complete all coursework, lab work, industrial training (6th semester), and a final year project to be eligible for the degree.

question: Are there any diploma courses available?
answer: Yes, we offer diploma programs in: Computer Science, Electronics, Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, and Pharmacy. Diploma programs are 3 years (6 semesters). We also offer 1-year PG Diploma programs in Data Science, Digital Marketing, and Hospital Management.

question: What specializations are available in MBA?
answer: MBA specializations include: Finance, Marketing, Human Resource Management, Information Technology, Operations Management, International Business, and Entrepreneurship. Students choose their specialization at the end of the 2nd semester based on preference and available seats.

question: Is there a PhD program?
answer: Yes, PhD programs are available in all major departments including Computer Science, Electronics, Mechanical, Civil, Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, Economics, and Management. Admission requires a valid UGC-NET/GATE score and a research proposal. The minimum duration is 3 years.

question: What is the medium of instruction?
answer: The primary medium of instruction is English for all professional and science programs. For Arts and Humanities programs, both English and Hindi medium options are available. All examinations are conducted in English, though students may write answers in Hindi for select non-professional courses.

question: What is the fee structure for B.Tech?
answer: B.Tech fee structure per year: Tuition Fee - Rs. 85,000, Development Fee - Rs. 15,000, Laboratory Fee - Rs. 10,000, Library Fee - Rs. 5,000, Exam Fee - Rs. 3,000, Student Activity Fee - Rs. 2,000. Total: Rs. 1,20,000 per year. One-time admission fee of Rs. 10,000 is payable at the time of admission.

question: What is the fee for MBA program?
answer: MBA fee structure per year: Tuition Fee - Rs. 1,50,000, Development Fee - Rs. 20,000, Library Fee - Rs. 5,000, Computer Lab Fee - Rs. 10,000, Industry Visit Fee - Rs. 5,000, Exam Fee - Rs. 5,000. Total: Rs. 1,95,000 per year. Additional one-time fees include admission fee (Rs. 15,000) and caution deposit (Rs. 10,000, refundable).

question: Is there an installment facility for fee payment?
answer: Yes, fees can be paid in two installments per semester. The first installment (60%) is due at the beginning of the semester, and the second installment (40%) is due by the mid-semester date. A nominal processing fee of Rs. 500 applies for installment payments. Education loan assistance is also available through partner banks.

question: What is the hostel fee?
answer: Hostel fees per year: Room Rent (shared, 2-seater) - Rs. 30,000, Room Rent (single occupancy) - Rs. 50,000, Mess Charges - Rs. 48,000 (Rs. 4,000/month), Electricity & Water - Rs. 6,000, Maintenance - Rs. 3,000, Caution Deposit - Rs. 5,000 (one-time, refundable). Total for shared room: approximately Rs. 87,000 per year.

question: Are there any fee concessions?
answer: Fee concessions are available for: 1) SC/ST students - 100% tuition fee waiver (government scheme), 2) OBC students - 50% tuition fee concession, 3) Single girl child - 25% fee concession, 4) Siblings studying in the same institution - 10% concession for second child, 5) Economically weaker sections (family income < 2.5 LPA) - up to 75% fee waiver.

question: What is the fee refund policy?
answer: Fee refund policy: Before class commencement - 100% refund (minus Rs. 1,000 processing fee), Within 15 days of class start - 80% refund, Within 30 days - 60% refund, Within 60 days - 40% refund, After 60 days - No refund. Caution deposits are refunded in full at the time of course completion or withdrawal.

question: What scholarships are available?
answer: Available scholarships: 1) Merit Scholarship - Top 5% students get 50-100% tuition waiver, 2) Sports Scholarship - up to 75% fee waiver for national/state level athletes, 3) Government Scholarships (SC/ST/OBC/Minority), 4) Need-based Financial Aid - for family income < 3 LPA, 5) Alumni Endowment Scholarships, 6) Industry-sponsored scholarships from TCS, Infosys, and Wipro for top performers.

question: How do I apply for a scholarship?
answer: Scholarship application process: 1) Fill the scholarship application form (available online and at the scholarship office), 2) Attach required documents: income certificate, mark sheets, caste certificate (if applicable), sports certificates, 3) Submit by the deadline (usually August 31 for odd semester, January 31 for even semester), 4) Shortlisted candidates will be called for verification, 5) Results announced within 30 days of deadline.

question: What is the merit scholarship criteria?
answer: Merit scholarship criteria: Students scoring above 90% in the previous semester/year exam receive 100% tuition fee waiver. Students scoring 80-90% receive 50% tuition fee waiver. Students scoring 75-80% receive 25% tuition fee waiver. The scholarship is renewed each semester based on continued academic performance. Minimum attendance of 85% is required to maintain the scholarship.

question: Are there government scholarships for minority students?
answer: Yes, the following government scholarships are available for minority students: 1) Central Sector Scholarship for Minorities (Rs. 20,000/year for UG), 2) Maulana Azad National Fellowship (for research scholars), 3) State Minority Welfare Scholarships, 4) Pre-Matric and Post-Matric Scholarships. The college scholarship office assists with application processing for all government schemes.

question: What are the hostel facilities?
answer: Hostel facilities include: Wi-Fi connectivity in all rooms, 24/7 security with CCTV surveillance, common room with TV and indoor games, gym and fitness center, laundry service, parking facility, reading room open 24/7, solar-heated water supply, RO drinking water, backup power generator, medical room with first-aid, and a mini convenience store on campus.

question: What are the hostel timings and rules?
answer: Hostel timings: Entry gate closes at 9:00 PM (weekdays) and 10:00 PM (weekends). Visitors are allowed from 4:00 PM to 7:00 PM in the visitor's lounge only. Overnight stay of guests is not permitted. Use of electrical appliances (heaters, irons) in rooms is restricted. Ragging in any form is strictly prohibited and punishable by expulsion. Alcohol and smoking are strictly banned on campus.

question: How do I apply for hostel accommodation?
answer: Hostel application process: 1) Apply online through the student portal after receiving admission confirmation, 2) Hostel allotment is on first-come-first-served basis, 3) Priority is given to outstation students (distance > 50 km from campus), 4) Pay hostel fee and caution deposit, 5) Room allotment details are sent via email and SMS. Apply early as rooms fill up fast — typically by mid-July.

question: Is there a mess facility?
answer: Yes, the hostel has a centralized mess facility serving vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals. Timings: Breakfast 7:30-9:00 AM, Lunch 12:30-2:00 PM, Snacks 4:30-5:30 PM, Dinner 7:30-9:00 PM. The menu rotates weekly and is prepared by professional catering staff. Special diet options are available on request. Mess charges are Rs. 4,000/month and are included in the hostel fee.

question: Are there separate hostels for boys and girls?
answer: Yes, there are separate hostel blocks for boys and girls with independent wardens and security. Boys' hostel has 4 blocks (capacity: 800 students), Girls' hostel has 3 blocks (capacity: 600 students). Each block has a resident warden. The girls' hostel has additional security measures including biometric entry and dedicated female security guards 24/7.

question: What is the placement record?
answer: Placement highlights for 2024-25: Overall placement rate - 87%, Highest package - Rs. 42 LPA (offered by Google), Average package - Rs. 6.8 LPA, Median package - Rs. 5.2 LPA. Total companies visited: 150+, Total offers made: 1,200+. B.Tech CSE has the highest placement rate at 95%, followed by ECE at 88% and IT at 90%.

question: Which companies recruit from the campus?
answer: Top recruiting companies include: IT/Software - TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Cognizant, Accenture, Capgemini, Tech Mahindra, L&T Infotech; Product Companies - Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Flipkart, Paytm, Zomato; Core Companies - Tata Motors, L&T, Siemens, ABB, Bosch; Consulting - Deloitte, EY, KPMG; Banking/Finance - ICICI, HDFC, Axis Bank. The placement cell organizes both on-campus and off-campus drives throughout the year.

question: When does the placement season begin?
answer: The placement season has two phases: 1) Pre-Placement Talks (PPTs) start in July-August for final year students, 2) Main placement drives run from September to March, 3) Dream company drives (packages > 10 LPA) are held in September-November, 4) Regular drives continue through March, 5) Off-campus placement support continues until June for unplaced students.

question: What placement training is provided?
answer: The college provides comprehensive placement training from the 5th semester: 1) Aptitude and reasoning classes, 2) Group discussion and personal interview workshops, 3) Resume building and LinkedIn profile optimization, 4) Technical mock interviews by industry professionals, 5) Soft skills and communication training, 6) Coding bootcamps for IT students (DSA, competitive programming), 7) Industry certification courses in AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

question: Are internships mandatory?
answer: Yes, internships are mandatory for all professional courses. B.Tech students must complete a 6-8 week summer internship after the 6th semester. MBA students have a 2-month summer internship after the 2nd semester. The college placement cell assists in securing internships with partner companies. Students can also arrange self-sourced internships with prior approval from the department.

question: How is the examination system structured?
answer: The examination system follows a Continuous Internal Assessment (CIA) model: Internal Assessment - 40% (mid-semester exams, assignments, quizzes, presentations), End Semester Exam - 60%. Mid-semester exams are held in the 8th and 14th week. End semester exams are conducted in November/December (odd semester) and April/May (even semester). Minimum 40% is required in both internal and external components to pass.

question: What is the grading system?
answer: The college follows a 10-point CGPA grading system: O (Outstanding) = 10, A+ = 9, A = 8, B+ = 7, B = 6, C = 5, P (Pass) = 4, F (Fail) = 0. Minimum CGPA of 4.0 is required for degree completion. Students with CGPA above 8.5 are awarded 'Distinction', and above 9.0 receive 'Distinction with Honours'.

question: What happens if I fail a subject?
answer: If you fail a subject, you can appear for a supplementary exam held within 2 months of the main exam results. You are allowed a maximum of 3 attempts per subject. If you fail in more than 3 subjects in a semester, you may be required to repeat the semester. Re-examination fee is Rs. 500 per subject. Back papers do not affect your ability to attend the next semester classes.

question: What is the minimum attendance required?
answer: Minimum 75% attendance is mandatory for all courses to be eligible to appear in end-semester exams. Students with 65-74% attendance may be allowed to appear with a fine of Rs. 1,000 and a letter to parents. Below 65% attendance results in being debarred from the end-semester exam, and the student must repeat the subject. Medical leave with valid certificates is considered for attendance relaxation.

question: How can I contact the admissions office?
answer: Admissions Office Contact: Phone - 011-XXXX-XXXX (Ext. 101), Toll-Free Helpline - 1800-XXX-XXXX, Email - admissions@college.edu, WhatsApp - +91-98XXX-XXXXX. Office Hours: Monday to Friday 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM. Office Location: Ground Floor, Administrative Block, Main Campus.

question: Where is the college located?
answer: The college is located at: Main Campus - 123 University Road, Knowledge Park, City - 110001. The campus is well-connected by public transport: Nearest Metro Station - University Metro (500m walk), Bus Routes - 101, 205, 340 stop at College Gate, Nearest Railway Station - City Junction (3 km). Free shuttle service is available from the metro station during college hours.

question: What are the library timings?
answer: Library Timings: Regular days - 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM, Exam period - 8:00 AM to 11:00 PM, Sundays and holidays - 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The digital library is accessible 24/7 through the student portal. The library has a collection of 50,000+ books, 200+ journals, and access to online databases including IEEE, Springer, and JSTOR.

question: Is there a student grievance cell?
answer: Yes, the Student Grievance Redressal Cell handles all student complaints. You can submit grievances: 1) Online through the student portal under 'Grievance' section, 2) Email - grievance@college.edu, 3) Drop box available at the Administrative Block entrance, 4) Meet the Grievance Officer during office hours (Mon-Fri, 2-4 PM). All grievances are acknowledged within 48 hours and resolved within 15 working days.

question: How do I get my ID card and email?
answer: Student ID cards are issued within the first week of admission. Collect from the Student Affairs Office with your admission receipt. College email IDs (student@college.edu) are activated within 3 working days of admission confirmation. Login credentials are sent to your registered mobile number via SMS. For any issues, contact the IT Helpdesk at it.support@college.edu or Extension 301.

question: What sports facilities are available?
answer: Sports facilities include: Cricket ground, Football field, Basketball and Volleyball courts, Tennis courts, Badminton courts (indoor), Table Tennis room, Athletics track (400m), Swimming pool (Olympic size), Gymnasium with modern equipment, Yoga and meditation hall. Annual sports meet 'Sportanza' is held in February. The college teams regularly participate in inter-university and national-level competitions.

question: Are there any student clubs and societies?
answer: Active student clubs include: Technical - Coding Club, Robotics Club, AI/ML Society, Hackathon Club; Cultural - Drama Society, Music Club, Dance Club, Photography Club, Film Making Club; Literary - Debating Society, Quiz Club, Creative Writing Club; Social - NSS, NCC, Rotaract Club, Environment Club. Annual cultural festival 'Euphoria' is held in March with 5,000+ participants from colleges across the country.

question: Is there a medical facility on campus?
answer: Yes, the campus has a Health Center with: 1) A qualified doctor available Mon-Sat (9 AM - 5 PM), 2) Nursing staff available 24/7, 3) Emergency first-aid facility, 4) Ambulance service for emergencies, 5) Tie-up with City Hospital (2 km away) for serious cases. All students are covered under the Group Medical Insurance scheme (premium included in fees) covering up to Rs. 1,00,000 per year.

question: Is Wi-Fi available on campus?
answer: Yes, the entire campus is Wi-Fi enabled with high-speed internet (100 Mbps). Students can connect using their college credentials. Wi-Fi is available in all academic buildings, hostels, library, and common areas. Each student gets 2 GB daily data limit (unlimited during 11 PM - 6 AM). Access to social media is restricted in academic buildings during class hours (9 AM - 4 PM).
